title: OSI, TCP/IP, Encapsulation, and Traffic Evidence · Protocol Recall
summary: Trace a client request through host, switch, router, firewall, service, and return path using captures and diagrams. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a layered packet-flow diagnosis after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain the responsibilities and failure evidence of all seven OSI reference-model layers.
Map TCP/IP link, internet, transport, and application functions to OSI layers without treating either as packet reality.
Trace data, segments or datagrams, packets, frames, and bits through encapsulation, forwarding, and decapsulation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
Use a changed review case derived from this module: network models flows. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Inspect the Net Packet Capture Limits trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for interpret frame, packet, transport, and application capture fields while accounting for vantage point, encryption, offload, and missing traffic.

content.text: For Net Packet Capture Limits, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Net Packet Capture Limits precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.
Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network models flows. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: One packet capture contains every hop and decrypted application payload automatically.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Packet Capture Limits applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Interpret frame, packet, transport, and application capture fields while accounting for vantage point, encryption, offload, and missing traffic.
